item.title: Bahrain Says Iran Hit A Desalination Plant, Stoking Fears Of Attacks On Civilian Sites
item.description: Bahrain has accused Iran of striking a desalination plant as the regional war expands and targets more civilian sites in Gulf states.
item.content:encoded: Bahrain has accused Iran of striking a desalination plant as the regional war expands and targets more civilian sites in Gulf states.

item.title: Explosion Hits U.S. Embassy In Oslo, Causing Minor Damage, Police Say
item.description: It was not immediately clear what caused the blast or who was involved, the Oslo police department said in a separate statement.
item.content:encoded: It was not immediately clear what caused the blast or who was involved, the Oslo police department said in a separate statement.

item.title: Kuwait Declares Force Majeure, Cuts Crude Oil Output Due To Middle East Conflict
item.description: Analysts predict the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia will have to also cut output soon as they run out of oil storage.
item.content:encoded: Analysts predict the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia will have to also cut output soon as they run out of oil storage.

item.title: Nightclub Bombing In Peru Injures 33, Including Minors
item.description: Among the injured are three minors: one 16-year-old and two 17-year-olds, according to the executive director of the Trujillo Health Network.
item.content:encoded: Among the injured are three minors: one 16-year-old and two 17-year-olds, according to the executive director of the Trujillo Health Network.

item.title: New Strikes Light Up The Night In Tehran As Israel Vows 'Many Surprises'
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Saturday night that Israel has “an organized plan with many surprises” for the next stage of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Saturday night that Israel has “an organized plan with many surprises” for the next stage of the war in Iran.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Will Be ‘Hit Very Hard' On Saturday
item.description: Trump added that he was considering widening the areas and groups of people being, targeted, without providing details.
item.content:encoded: Trump added that he was considering widening the areas and groups of people being, targeted, without providing details.

item.title: Russian Flag Returns As Milan Cortina Winter Paralympics Open Amid Iran War And Boycott
item.description: The Milan Cortina Winter Paralympics has officially opened with some countries boycotting the opening ceremony because of the return of the Russian flag to the global sports stage.
item.content:encoded: The Milan Cortina Winter Paralympics has officially opened with some countries boycotting the opening ceremony because of the return of the Russian flag to the global sports stage.

item.title: BlackRock Fund Limits Withdrawals As Redemptions Rattle Private Credit
item.description: Shares of the world's largest asset manager fell 6.7% on the New York Stock Exchange.
item.content:encoded: Shares of the world's largest asset manager fell 6.7% on the New York Stock Exchange.

item.title: Russia Provided Iran With Information That Can Help Tehran Strike U.S. Military: AP Report
item.description: It's the first indication that Moscow has sought to get involved in the war that the U.S. and Israel launched on Iran a week ago.
item.content:encoded: It's the first indication that Moscow has sought to get involved in the war that the U.S. and Israel launched on Iran a week ago.

item.title: U.S. Issues A License That Authorizes Sales Of Venezuelan Gold
item.description: The license was issued after U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum met in Venezuela with acting President Delcy Rodríguez this week.
item.content:encoded: The license was issued after U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum met in Venezuela with acting President Delcy Rodríguez this week.

item.title: Stocks Slide As Oil Price Fears, U.S. Jobs Data Rattle Markets
item.description: Oil prices hit their highest levels in almost two years, and Qatari officials warn they could spike as high as $150 per barrel.
item.content:encoded: Oil prices hit their highest levels in almost two years, and Qatari officials warn they could spike as high as $150 per barrel.
